data.title: SEC's Crypto Task Force arranges nationwide roundtables to engage with early-stage crypto projects, promoting direct interaction; starting August 4 in Berkeley, California, and ending December 5 in Ann Arbor, Michigan; aiming to include voices traditionally excluded from policymaking.
data.description: The SEC's Crypto Task Force, overseen by Commissioner Hester Peirce, is broadening its reach nationally with upcoming roundtable discussions in key U.S. cities. This initiative is geared towards enhancing direct engagement between the SEC and small, early-stage crypto ventures that are typically left out of formal policymaking processes. The "Crypto Task Force on the Road" tour, starting on August 4 in Berkeley, California, and wrapping up on December 5 in Ann Arbor, Michigan, will also make stops in Boston, Dallas, Chicago, New York City, Irvine, Cleveland, and Scottsdale. This move aims to gather feedback from individuals who couldn't attend previous roundtables.
Chief Canadian Negotiator in trade talks with US: I will talk to US Commerce Secretary Lutnick next week and seek a meeting later in August.

data.title: Trump threatens Russia with nuclear escalation, causing Wall Street fear gauge to spike. 3 destabilizing headlines, nuclear submarines moved. SPY ETF soaring.
data.description: August, known for its volatility, began with a tumultuous start in 2025 on Wall Street. The SPY ETF is displaying rapid movement, emphasizing the unpredictable nature of the market. Within a day, investors faced significant challenges - President Donald Trump announced new tariffs, a disappointing U.S. jobs report was released, and tensions rose with Russia over nuclear discussions. In response to provocative remarks from former Russian President Dmitry Medvedev, Trump ordered the strategic placement of two Nuclear Submarines, citing the importance of cautious language to prevent unforeseen outcomes. For more detailed information, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Introducing Google's Veo 3: An AI tool revolutionizing image-to-video creation, empowering creators with improved visual storytelling.
data.description: Google has introduced Veo 3, a state-of-the-art AI tool that converts images into videos, providing creators with greater control over visual narratives. This tool is designed to enhance the storytelling process with advanced features.

data.title: Autoket rebrands as Supply Veins, an AI-powered communications platform for B2B procurement.Miami, Aug. 1, 2025/PRNewswire.
data.description: The CEO and Co-Founder of Supply Veins is a graduate of the PenFed Foundation for Military Heroes Veteran Entrepreneur Program. Autoket, a B2B marketplace for automotive fleet parts powered by AI, revealed its extensive rebrand on Aug. 1, 2025, in Miami.

data.title: ResMed's Q2 earnings exceed expectations: $2.55 per share (est. $2.46) and sales $1.348B (est. $1.321B). Stock up 2.1% at $277.70.
data.description: Resmed Inc (NYSE: RMD) exceeded expectations in Q2 with earnings of $2.55 per share, surpassing the predicted $2.46 per share. Sales for the quarter reached $1.348 billion, exceeding the expected $1.321 billion. Resmed's Chairman and CEO, Mick Farrell, attributed the successful quarter to strong global demand for their sleep and breathing health devices. Following the report, ResMed shares increased by 2.1%, reaching $277.70 on Friday. For more details, refer to the full story available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Tesla found partially responsible for a fatal crash in 2019, as per lawyers claiming Autopilot could have prevented it.
data.description: Attorneys representing the family of a woman who died after being hit by a Tesla sedan in 2019 claim that the vehicle's Autopilot system could have prevented the accident. This incident raised concerns over the safety of Tesla's technology and highlighted the importance of implementing more advanced safety measures in autonomous vehicles. The tragic event underscores the need for continuous improvement in self-driving car technologies to prevent similar accidents in the future.

data.title: UNITS® Moving and Portable Storage sponsors Timmy Hill's #56 Toyota at Watkins Glen NASCAR Truck Series race on August 8.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, UNITS® Moving and Portable Storage will have their name featured prominently on Hill Motorsports' #56 Toyota as the primary sponsor for a top race event in Watkins Glen, New York. UNITS®, known for their creative moving and storage services, will be showcased during this high-profile event.

data.title: MicroStrategy beats Q2 revenue estimates at $114.49M, EPS at $32.52/share. Introduced Treasury Preferred Stock.
data.description: MicroStrategy Inc (NASDAQ: MSTR) revealed strong sales performance in the second quarter. Their revenue for this period stood at $114.49 million, surpassing the anticipated $112.96 million as per analysts. The company's adjusted earnings per share for Q2 were reported at $32.52. Michael Saylor, the executive chairman, highlighted the introduction of the world's initial Treasury Preferred Stock during the July IPO of STRC, aimed at offering stability in pricing and generating high-yield to a fresh group of investors. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Boot Barn (BOOT) exceeded Q1 expectations, with earnings at $1.74/share and revenue at $504.067M. FY26 EPS guidance raised to $5.80-$6.70 and sales guidance to $2.100B-$2.180B. CEO John Hazen optimistic about the company's performance.
data.description: Boot Barn Holdings Inc (NYSE: BOOT) announced robust first-quarter results, surpassing expectations. Earnings stood at $1.74 per share, beating the projected $1.52 per share. The company's quarterly sales were $504.067 million, exceeding the anticipated $492.339 million. Consequently, Boot Barn raised its FY2026 guidance, with GAAP EPS now in the range of $5.80 to $6.70 (up from $5.50-$6.40) and sales expected to reach $2.100 billion-$2.180 billion (previously $2.070 billion-$2.150 billion). CEO John Hazen expressed satisfaction with the strong start to fiscal 2026. The full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: AbbVie's Q2 2025 sales hit $15.42B, surpassing estimates. Adjusted earnings at $2.97, raising FY guidance. Humira sales down.
data.description: Pharmaceutical company AbbVie Inc. (NYSE: ABBV) announced their second-quarter sales for 2025 at $15.42 billion, surpassing the expected consensus of $14.98 billion. The sales saw a 6.6% increase on a reported basis or 6.5% operationally. The company also revealed adjusted earnings of $2.97, marking a 12.1% rise from the previous year but falling short of the estimated $3.23. AbbVie adjusted their fiscal 2025 earnings guidance from $11.67-$11.87 per share to $11.88-$12.08, as opposed to the analyst forecast of $12.24. Management hinted at a probable decrease in net pricing in the latter part of the year, while highlighting a positive volume growth trend for their products. This information was reported through Benzinga.com which also covered President Trump's 60-day ultimatum to US pharmaceutical companies to reduce drug prices.

data.title: Wayfair to announce quarterly earnings on 2025-08-04, with expected EPS of $0.08. Hopes high for surpassing estimates.
data.description: Home goods retailer Wayfair (NYSE: W) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on Monday, August 4, 2025. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.08. The market eagerly anticipates the results, with a strong desire for surpassing expectations and positive guidance for the following quarter. Investors are advised to stay informed as the announcement could impact trading decisions.
